focus: [D2-11/D2] command metadata drift - derive /design:* command metadata from one source and build-validate wrapper parity

graphEvents.label: Where should /design:* command descriptions, argument hints and aliases live so wrappers can be generated or drift-checked?
Current /design:* wrappers hand-repeat descriptions and generic argument hints while aliases live elsewhere
sk-design mode-registry is the routing/advisor source of truth, not a full command-surface schema
impeccable-main uses command-metadata.json as the single source for command descriptions and argument hints read by build and pin surfaces
Command wrappers must derive from or be validated against one command metadata source that points to registry mode keys

label: Current /design:* wrappers repeat command descriptions and generic argument hints by hand; no source-backed command schema can validate them

mode-registry.json is currently a routing/advisor source of truth, while command descriptions and argument hints live outside it; command metadata should point to registry mode keys rather than duplicate mode logic
impeccable-main provides the source-first pattern: command-metadata.json owns command descriptions and argument hints, while build/provider surfaces consume generated metadata
Every command-visible /design surface must have exactly one command metadata record whose ownerMode matches a mode-registry workflowMode, and wrapper frontmatter must derive from or match that record

The current prompt premise is slightly overstated: aliases are not present in command frontmatter and argument-hint is not present in mode-registry.json; the verified issue is fragmented metadata, not exact triplication

direction: Put full command descriptions, argument hints, aliases, examples and proof fields directly in hub SKILL prose
reason: The hub explicitly stays routing-only/no per-mode design logic, and prose cannot be reliably drift-checked against wrappers without becoming another duplicated authoring surface.
